Remediation: Exchange admin center > Settings > Modern authentication > Enable. Run: Set-OrganizationConfig -OAuth2ClientProfileEnabled $true
Run: Set-RemoteDomain -Identity Default -AutoForwardEnabled $false. Also consider transport rules to block client-side forwarding.
Exchange admin center > Organization > Sharing > Default sharing policy. Remove wildcard (*) domains or restrict to CalendarSharingFreeBusySimple.
Run: Set-MailboxAuditBypassAssociation -Identity <user> -AuditBypassEnabled $false for each bypassed mailbox.
Run: Set-TransportConfig -SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled $true. Disable SMTP AUTH org-wide and enable per-mailbox only where required.

Setting: Outlook Add-ins Allowed (Default Role Assignment Policy)
Remediation: Exchange admin center > Roles > User roles > Default Role Assignment Policy. Remove MyMarketplaceApps, MyCustomApps, MyReadWriteMailboxApps roles.
Run: Set-HostedConnectionFilterPolicy -Identity <Policy> -IPAllowList @{}. Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and remove all IP allow list entries.
Run: Set-HostedConnectionFilterPolicy -Identity <Policy> -EnableSafeList $false. Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and uncheck "Turn on safe list".

CurrentValue: All 1 sampled mailboxes have auditing enabled
